articles.title: ✅ TESTING MILESTONES CHECKLIST
articles.summary: When you're just starting out, you're barely testing at all. As you start to think more about scale and rigor, tests become more habitual. As your code and team mature, testing becomes a way of life.

articles.title: HOW WE BUILT THE WORLD'S FASTEST VIN DECODER
articles.summary: Cardog built the world's fastest VIN decoder by optimizing the NHTSA's VPIC database, which contains 40 years of vehicle data but doesn't have performance optimization. It achieved this by stripping regulatory metadata, removing unused patterns, and applying modern SQLite optimization techniques, reducing the database size from 1.5GB to 21MB. This resulted in a huge performance improvement, with response times decreasing from 3.2 seconds to under 30ms and eliminating network dependency.

articles.title: PARSING PROTOBUF LIKE NEVER BEFORE
articles.summary: hyperpb is a new high-performance and runtime-dynamic Protobuf parsing library for Go. It uses a dynamic approach, compiling code at runtime to enable online profile-guided optimization for parsing efficiency. This article goes over its architecture, focusing on its VM-based parser, memory management, and unique optimizations like zerocopy strings and arena reuse.

articles.title: DEVELOPERS, NOT OPERATORS
articles.summary: AI is not reinventing developers, and adopting AI tools is not the only path to success. It's important that devs have the freedom to choose their own tools and approaches. Software development is more than just generating code quickly - it requires understanding problems, designing solutions, and making architectural decisions.

articles.title: HTML IS DEAD, LONG LIVE HTML
articles.summary: The DOM and CSS are outdated and inefficient for modern web apps due to their complexity, legacy issues, and the blurring of UI and content. Common problems include the stringly-typed nature of the DOM, CSS' inside-out approach to layout, and the inadequacy of text manipulation APIs. Alternatives like "HTML in Canvas" and GPU-based renderers are promising to look into.

articles.title: IT'S TIME FOR MODERN CSS TO KILL THE SPA
articles.summary: Modern CSS features like the View Transitions API and Speculation Rules have made Single Page Applications (SPAs) largely unnecessary by enabling smooth page transitions and instant navigation without requiring heavy JavaScript frameworks. While SPAs were originally adopted to create app-like experiences, browsers can now do what SPAs do natively with simple CSS.

articles.title: CLAUDE CODE IDE INTEGRATION FOR EMACS
articles.summary: Claude Code IDE for Emacs is an integration that allows the Claude Code CLI to interact with Emacs' features through the Model Context Protocol (MCP).  It provides a bidirectional bridge enabling Claude to understand and leverage Emacs functionalities like LSP, project management, and custom Elisp functions. The IDE comes with automatic project detection, terminal integration, MCP tool support for accessing Emacs commands, diagnostic integration, and advanced diff views.

articles.title: ELEVENLABS REACT NATIVE SDK
articles.summary: The ElevenLabs Conversational AI SDK allows devs to integrate interactive voice agents into React Native applications. It requires React Native with LiveKit dependencies, microphone permissions, and development builds for Expo compatibility.

articles.title: STATICSEARCH
articles.summary: StaticSearch is a client-side JavaScript search engine designed for static websites. It indexes website content to create JSON and JavaScript data files for search functionality. The engine has a web component for easy integration, along with modules and APIs for more customized implementations.

articles.title: WRITING A RUST GPU KERNEL DRIVER: A BRIEF INTRODUCTION ON HOW GPU DRIVERS WORK
articles.summary: This article teaches the basics of GPU driver architecture, focusing on the user-mode driver (UMD) and kernel-mode driver (KMD) relationship using an example of rendering a rotating cube with Vulkan (VkCube). The UMD translates high-level API commands into lower-level GPU instructions, while the KMD manages memory allocation, job scheduling, and hardware initialization. The Tyr driver, a Rust-based KMD, provides an interface for device information, memory management, job submission, and tiler heap management.

articles.title: WHY BUILDING BILLING SYSTEMS IS SO PAINFUL
articles.summary: There are many hidden complexities of building billing systems, far beyond simple subscription models. Billing involves managing various pricing models, real-time usage tracking, and adapting to changing needs. Billing is not just invoicing, but a critical infrastructure that impacts the entire business, so it often requires a specialized team with expertise in both engineering and finance.

articles.title: JULES, OUR ASYNCHRONOUS CODING AGENT, IS NOW AVAILABLE FOR EVERYONE
articles.summary: Jules, an asynchronous coding agent powered by Gemini 2.5, is now open to the public.

articles.title: AI IN SEARCH: DRIVING MORE QUERIES AND HIGHER QUALITY CLICKS
articles.summary: AI-powered search features on Google are leading to more user queries and a higher quality of clicks to websites, still in the range of billions of clicks a day.

articles.title: LOGICAL ASSIGNMENT OPERATORS IN JAVASCRIPT: SMALL SYNTAX, BIG WINS
articles.summary: JavaScript's logical assignment operators (`||=`, `&&=`, `??=`) offer concise syntax for conditionally assigning values based on logical checks (falsy, truthy, or nullish).

articles.title: PGLINE
articles.summary: Pgline is a high-performance TypeScript PostgreSQL driver for Node.js using pipeline mode with faster speed and lower CPU usage in benchmarks compared to Bun SQL, Postgres.js, and node-postgres.
